Australia.notes: Australia citizens enter their own country without a visa.

Canada.notes: Australian citizens are visa-exempt and require an Electronic Travel Authorization (eTA) to fly to Canada; eTA costs CAD $7 and is valid for up to 5 years.

France.notes: Australian citizens are authorized to enter and stay in France without a visa for 90 days within any 180-day period under Schengen rules.

Germany.notes: Australian citizens are visa-free for up to 90 days in a 180-day period under a bilateral visa exemption agreement.

Greece.notes: Australian passport holders may enter Greece visa-free for tourism purposes for 90 days within any 6-month period.

Indonesia.notes: Electronic Visitor Visa (e-VoA) single-entry; 30-day stay permitted; costs IDR 500,000.

Italy.notes: Australian citizens can travel to Italy visa-free for tourism for up to 90 days within the Schengen Area.

Japan.notes: Australian citizens may enter Japan visa-free for tourism and stay up to 90 days.

Malaysia.notes: Australian passport holders receive visa-free entry for up to 90 days for tourism; passport must be valid 6+ months with two blank pages.

Mexico.notes: Australian passport holders can enter Mexico visa-free for up to 180 days for tourism purposes with no advance visa application required.

Netherlands.notes: Australian passport holders are visa-free for the Netherlands and can stay up to 90 days in any 180-day period within the Schengen area.

Singapore.notes: Australian passport holders do not require a visa for tourist visits; they receive a Visit Pass upon arrival valid for up to 90 days.

South Korea.notes: Australian passport holders are exempt from K-ETA until 31 December 2026; can stay visa-free for up to 90 days.

Spain.notes: Australian citizens are visa-free to enter Spain (Schengen area) for up to 90 days within any 180-day period for tourism.

Switzerland.notes: Australian citizens are visa-free to Switzerland since 1 January 2022; 90-day limit per 180-day period in Schengen Area.

Thailand.notes: Australian tourists can enter Thailand visa-free for up to 60 days for tourism purposes; extendable for additional 30 days at immigration office.

Turkey.notes: Ordinary Australian passport holders are exempt from visa requirements for tourism and transit for up to 90 days within any 180-day period (effective April 17, 2026).

United Arab Emirates.notes: Australian citizens can enter the UAE visa-free for 90 days per 180-day period with no advance visa required.

United Kingdom.notes: Australian passport holders require an Electronic Travel Authorisation (ETA) to visit the UK for tourism, visiting family, or business for up to 6 months.

United States.notes: Australian citizens eligible for Visa Waiver Program; ESTA approval required before travel, no visa needed.

Australia.notes: Canadian citizens require an Electronic Travel Authority (ETA); allows multiple entries with up to 90 days per stay within a 12-month period.

Canada.notes: Canada citizens enter their own country without a visa.

France.notes: Visa-free for up to 90 days in any 180-day period within the Schengen area.

Germany.notes: Canadian citizens can stay visa-free in Germany for up to 90 days within any 180-day period as tourists.

Greece.notes: Canadian citizens do not require a visa for stays up to 90 days in any 180-day period in Greece as a Schengen area visitor.

Indonesia.notes: Canadian passport holders must apply online for e-Visitor Visa allowing 30-day single-entry stay for tourism.

Italy.notes: Canadian citizens are exempt from visa requirements for tourism stays up to 90 days in any 180-day period.

Japan.notes: Canadian passport holders can enter Japan visa-free for tourism up to 90 days.

Malaysia.notes: Canadian citizens do not require a tourist visa for stays up to 90 days.

Mexico.notes: Canadian citizens do not require a visa; visa-free entry for up to 180 days with valid passport and completed FMM form.

Netherlands.notes: Canadian citizens can visit the Netherlands visa-free for up to 90 days within any 180-day period as part of the Schengen area.

Singapore.notes: Canadian passport holders do not require a visa for tourist visits up to 30 days; SG Arrival Card completion required.

South Korea.notes: Canadian citizens are visa-free for tourism; K-ETA exemption extended through December 31, 2026.

Spain.notes: Canadian citizens exempt from visa; visa-free entry for up to 90 days in any 180-day period under Schengen rules.

Switzerland.notes: Canadian citizens do not require a visa for tourism; visa-free access is limited to 90 days per 180-day period within the Schengen area.

Thailand.notes: Canadian citizens can enter without a visa for tourism stays up to 60 days; extensions available at Thailand's Immigration Office.

Turkey.notes: Ordinary Canadian passport holders are visa-exempt for tourism visits up to 90 days within any 180-day period.

United Arab Emirates.notes: Canadian passport holders receive a 90-day visa on arrival at UAE ports of entry, valid for 6 months from issue date, free of charge.

United Kingdom.notes: Electronic Travel Authorization (eTA) required before travel; apply online at least 3 days before departure; tourist stays permitted up to 6 months.

United States.notes: Canadian citizens do not require a nonimmigrant visa for tourism; valid passport required.

Australia.notes: German passport holders require an eVisitor (Subclass 651) for tourism; allows 90-day stays, valid 12 months with multiple entries.

Canada.notes: German citizens require an eTA (electronic travel authorization) to enter Canada by air; normally permits stays up to 6 months.

France.notes: German EU citizens enjoy visa-free travel to France for short stays up to 90 days within any 180-day period.

Germany.notes: Germany citizens enter their own country without a visa.

Greece.notes: German citizens can travel visa-free to Greece as EU/Schengen nationals with no visa requirement.

Indonesia.notes: E-Visa on Arrival (eVOA) valid for single-entry, 30-day stay; apply online or at airport.

Italy.notes: German citizens are EU/Schengen members; visa-free entry with valid passport or identity card.

Japan.notes: German passport holders can enter Japan visa-free for tourism and short-term stays up to 90 days without prior visa application.

Malaysia.notes: German citizens do not require a visa to enter Malaysia as tourists and can stay visa-free for up to 90 days.

Mexico.notes: German citizens do not require a visa for tourism; enter visa-free with a valid passport and tourist card (FMM).

Netherlands.notes: German passport holders have visa-free access to the Netherlands as both countries are Schengen Area members; standard 90-day short-stay limit applies.

Singapore.notes: German nationals are not on Singapore's visa-required list and may enter visa-free; social visit pass typically granted for up to 90 days.

South Korea.notes: German citizens eligible for visa-free entry; K-ETA optional and exempted through December 31, 2026.

Spain.notes: EU citizens (German passport holders) may enter Spain visa-free with valid passport or national ID card valid for 3+ months; 90-day maximum stay in Schengen area.

Switzerland.notes: German (EU) citizens do not require a visa; visa-free entry for up to 90 days within any 180-day period.

Thailand.notes: German passport holders can enter Thailand visa-free for up to 60 days for tourism and business purposes.

Turkey.notes: German citizens exempt from visa for tourism; maximum 90 days within any 180-day period.

United Arab Emirates.notes: German citizens may enter the UAE visa-free for up to 90 days within any 180-day period with a valid passport (6+ months validity).

United Kingdom.notes: German citizens require a UK Electronic Travel Authorization (ETA, £20) valid for 2 years; permits tourism for up to 6 months.

United States.notes: German passport holders qualify for the Visa Waiver Program; requires approved ESTA before travel; permitted stay up to 90 days.

Australia.notes: Indian passport holders require a Visitor visa (subclass 600), applied online before travel; typically granted for up to 3 months.

Canada.notes: Indian passport holders require a Temporary Resident Visa (TRV) applied in advance; Canada does not offer visa-on-arrival.

France.notes: Schengen short-stay visa required; apply at French consulate in India with minimum 5 days notice.

Germany.notes: Indian citizens require a Schengen visa (Type C) for tourism; valid for up to 90 days within a 180-day period; must apply in advance, no visa-on-arrival.

Greece.notes: Short-stay Schengen visa required; apply in advance at the Global Visa Center (official service provider for Greece in India).

Indonesia.notes: Indian nationals can obtain a Visitor e-Visa for a single-entry 30-day stay; extendable to 60 days total.

Italy.notes: Indian nationals require a Schengen C visa applied through the Italian Embassy or VFS Global centers; valid for stays up to 90 days within 180 days.

Japan.notes: Indian nationals can apply for Japan e-visa (single-entry) for tourist visits up to 90 days, available through VFS Global visa application centers since April 2024.

Malaysia.notes: Indian nationals are visa-exempt for Malaysia until 31 December 2026 for tourism purposes; 30-day stay permitted with valid passport (6+ months validity) and Malaysia Digital Arrival Card submission.

Mexico.notes: Indian nationals require a visitor visa in advance from the Mexican Embassy; visa-free entry permitted only with valid US, Canada, Japan, UK, or Schengen visa.

Netherlands.notes: Indian passport holders require a Schengen visa (advance embassy application). Maximum 90 days in 180-day period.

Singapore.notes: Indian passport holders require an electronic visa (e-visa) to enter Singapore; apply online with S$30 fee, processing typically within 3 working days.

South Korea.notes: Indian nationals require a C-3-9 Tourist Visa and must apply in advance through official application centers.

Spain.notes: Schengen visa required; permits 90-day stay within any 180-day period.

Switzerland.notes: Schengen visa required for short stays up to 90 days within 180-day period; apply at Swiss embassy or consulate.

Thailand.notes: Indian passport holders receive 60-day visa-free entry for tourism and short-term business; extendable for 30 days in-country.

Turkey.notes: Indian passport holders eligible for e-visa if they possess valid visa/residence permit from Schengen, USA, UK, or Ireland countries. Apply at evisa.gov.tr.

United Arab Emirates.notes: Tourist visa required; must be arranged through UAE-based sponsor (hotel booking, airline, or travel agent) before travel.

United Kingdom.notes: Indian nationals require a Standard Visitor visa to enter the UK for tourism; maximum stay up to 6 months.

United States.notes: B-2 tourist visa required; apply at US Embassy or Consulate in India.

Australia.notes: Japanese passport holders can apply for an ETA (Electronic Travel Authority) to visit Australia for tourism; valid for up to 3 months per entry.

Canada.notes: Japanese citizens are visa-exempt and require Electronic Travel Authorization (eTA) to enter Canada by air; valid up to 5 years, CAD $7, typically approved within minutes.

France.notes: Japanese passport holders can enter France visa-free for up to 90 days within any 180-day period for tourism and business purposes.

Germany.notes: Japanese passport holders do not need a visa for tourist stays up to 90 days in Germany.

Greece.notes: Japanese citizens can enter Greece visa-free for up to 90 days within a 180-day period as part of Schengen Area exemption.

Indonesia.notes: Japan is eligible for 30-day Visitor Visa on Arrival at Indonesian ports of entry.

Italy.notes: Japanese citizens may enter Italy visa-free for tourism for up to 90 days within any 180-day period.

Japan.notes: Japan citizens enter their own country without a visa.

Malaysia.notes: Japanese citizens do not require a visa for tourist/social visits up to 90 days.

Mexico.notes: Japanese nationals are visa-free for tourism in Mexico for up to 180 days.

Netherlands.notes: Japanese nationals are visa-free for Netherlands as part of Schengen zone; max 90 days in any 180-day period.

Singapore.notes: Japanese nationals do not need to apply for a visa to travel to Singapore for leisure or business; 6-month passport validity required.

South Korea.notes: Visa-free entry for up to 90 days; K-ETA exemption for Japan extends through December 31, 2026.

Spain.notes: No Schengen visa required for Japanese passport holders; visa-free entry for up to 90 days.

Switzerland.notes: Visa-free entry for up to 90 days within a 180-day period for Japanese tourists.

Thailand.notes: Japanese passport holders can enter Thailand visa-free for up to 30 days for tourism under bilateral visa exemption agreement.

Turkey.notes: Japanese passport holders are exempt from visa requirements for tourist visits up to 90 days per 180-day period.

United Arab Emirates.notes: Japanese nationals with valid passports can enter the UAE visa-free for up to 90 days, effective from August 1, 2024.

United Kingdom.notes: Japanese passport holders require an Electronic Travel Authorisation (ETA) costing £20 for tourist visits up to 6 months.

United States.notes: Japanese citizens part of US Visa Waiver Program; must obtain ESTA before boarding air/sea carrier.

Australia.notes: Singapore passport holders are eligible for Australia's Electronic Travel Authority (ETA subclass 601); valid for 12 months with multiple entries, up to 90 days per visit.

Canada.notes: Singapore passport holders are visa-exempt and can enter Canada for up to 6 months with an eTA (required for air travel only).

France.notes: Singapore passport holders can enter France visa-free for up to 90 days within any 180-day period for tourism and business.

Germany.notes: Singapore citizens do not need a visa to enter Germany as tourists if staying 90 days or less within 6 months.

Greece.notes: Singapore passport holders are visa-free for Greece; can stay up to 90 days within any 180-day period in the Schengen area.

Indonesia.notes: Singapore citizens (ASEAN member) enjoy visa-free entry for up to 30 days for tourism; non-extendable.

Italy.notes: Singapore passport holders are exempt from Schengen visa requirements for short stays up to 90 days within 180 days.

Japan.notes: Singapore citizens are visa-exempt for up to 90 days for temporary visits (tourism, business, visiting friends/relatives).

Malaysia.notes: Singapore nationals do not require a visa for entry to Malaysia; visa-free for up to 30 days as ASEAN member state.

Mexico.notes: Singapore passport holders do not require a visa for Mexico; visa-free entry up to 180 days for tourism.

Netherlands.notes: Singapore citizens do not need a visa; visa-free entry for up to 90 days within 180 days in the Schengen area.

Singapore.notes: Singapore citizens enter their own country without a visa.

South Korea.notes: Singapore passport holders do not require a visa for short-term tourist entry to South Korea; visa-free stay permitted for up to 90 days.

Spain.notes: Singapore is visa-exempt for Schengen entry; tourists may stay up to 90 days within any 180-day period.

Switzerland.notes: Visa-free entry for up to 90 days within any 180-day period; passport valid 3+ months beyond departure.

Thailand.notes: Singapore citizens enjoy visa-free entry for 60 days as tourists; TDAC (Thailand Digital Arrival Card) submission required online within 3 days prior to arrival.

Turkey.notes: Singapore passport holders are visa-exempt for tourism up to 90 days within 180 days.

United Arab Emirates.notes: Singapore passport holders are exempt from visa requirements and receive a 30-day visit visa upon arrival.

United Kingdom.notes: Singapore passport holders must obtain a UK Electronic Travel Authorisation (ETA) online before travel to visit as a tourist for up to 6 months.

United States.notes: Singapore citizens qualify for the Visa Waiver Program and can visit the US for tourism for up to 90 days with ESTA approval.

Australia.notes: eVisitor visa required; apply online before travel for up to 3-month stays.

Canada.notes: UK citizens need an electronic travel authorization (eTA) to board flights to Canada; costs CAD $7 and is valid for up to 5 years. Valid for stays up to 6 months.

France.notes: UK passport holders can travel visa-free to France for up to 90 days in any 180-day period.

Germany.notes: UK citizens do not require a visa for tourism in Germany; visa-free stay limited to 90 days within any 180-day period under Schengen rules.

Greece.notes: UK passport holders can travel visa-free to Greece (Schengen area) for up to 90 days within any 180-day period.

Indonesia.notes: 30-day Visa on Arrival obtainable at major airports and ports for 500,000 Indonesian Rupiah; alternatively apply for e-visa online.

Italy.notes: UK passport holders can enter Italy visa-free for tourism for up to 90 days within any 180-day period.

Japan.notes: UK passport holders are visa-free for short-term tourism; 90-day visitor exemption stamp issued on arrival.

Malaysia.notes: UK citizens are visa-free; automatic social visit pass granted on arrival for up to 90 days.

Mexico.notes: UK passport holders enter visa-free for tourism and may stay up to 180 days; valid passport required.

Netherlands.notes: British passport holders do not require a visa to visit the Netherlands; visa-free entry permitted for up to 90 days within any 180-day period.

Singapore.notes: UK passport holders can enter Singapore visa-free for tourism; stay normally permitted between 30-90 days at immigration officer's discretion.

South Korea.notes: UK passport holders are visa-free for up to 90 days; K-ETA exemption extended through 31 December 2026; e-arrival card required.

Spain.notes: UK passport holders can visit visa-free for up to 90 days within any 180-day period; passport must be valid for at least 3 months beyond departure.

Switzerland.notes: UK passport holders do not require a visa for tourist stays up to 90 days in any 180-day period within the Schengen area.

Thailand.notes: UK passport holders can enter Thailand visa-free for up to 60 days; proof of return travel required.

Turkey.notes: UK ordinary passport holders are exempt from visas for up to 90 days in any 180-day period for tourism.

United Arab Emirates.notes: Free visa upon arrival valid for 90 days within a 180-day rolling period; no advance application required.

United Kingdom.notes: United Kingdom citizens enter their own country without a visa.

United States.notes: UK passport holders eligible for Visa Waiver Program; require ESTA approval before travel; maximum 90-day stay.

Australia.notes: US citizens must apply for an Electronic Travel Authority (ETA), valid for up to 90 days per entry within a 12-month period.

Canada.notes: US citizens do not need a visa or eTA; valid passport required for entry. Maximum stay 6 months.

France.notes: US citizens can stay up to 90 days within any 180-day period visa-free for tourism or business purposes.

Germany.notes: US citizens can stay up to 90 days without a visa; valid passport required with 3 months beyond departure.

Greece.notes: US citizens can visit Greece visa-free for up to 90 days within 180 days; valid passport required.

Indonesia.notes: Electronic Visa on Arrival (e-VOA) required, costs 500,000 rupiah (~$35), valid for 30 days from entry; extendable once for additional 30 days.

Italy.notes: US citizens may enter Italy for up to 90 days for tourist purposes without a visa under the Schengen area visa waiver.

Japan.notes: US citizens can enter Japan visa-free for up to 90 days for tourism; cannot work.

Malaysia.notes: US citizens do not require a visa for tourism stays up to 90 days; social visit pass issued on arrival.

Mexico.notes: No visa required for tourist visits; Mexican immigration determines authorized stay length upon arrival (typically 30–180 days).

Netherlands.notes: No visa required for tourist stays up to 90 days; part of Schengen Agreement visa waiver.

Singapore.notes: US passport holders do not require a visa for tourism; 90-day visa-free entry granted via electronic visit pass upon arrival.

South Korea.notes: US citizens may stay visa-free for 90 days or less for tourism or business.

Spain.notes: US citizens can visit Spain for up to 90 days in any 180-day period without a visa.

Switzerland.notes: US citizens may enter Switzerland visa-free for up to 90 days for tourism or business.

Thailand.notes: US citizens can enter visa-free for up to 60 days; pre-arrival online registration required.

Turkey.notes: US citizens exempt from visa for tourism stays up to 90 days in any 180-day period.

United Arab Emirates.notes: US passport holders are granted visa-free entry for up to 30 days; extension available at discretion of immigration officials.

United Kingdom.notes: US passport holders require an Electronic Travel Authorization (ETA) before travel; Standard Visitors may stay up to 6 months.

United States.notes: United States citizens enter their own country without a visa.
